title: Academic Figures MCP Workflow v2
goal: Create a strict repo-specific process flowchart that shows the current plan-first workflow exactly once per step: Input, Plan, Payload, Generate, Evaluate, Iterate, Output. The diagram must remove the duplicate PAYLOAD error from the previous version and keep the main path unambiguous.
visual_direction: strict, repo-specific, process-centric, highly legible technical flowchart with one unambiguous main path

source_context.summary: Input -> Plan -> Payload -> Generate -> Evaluate -> Iterate -> Output, with one feedback loop only

must_include: exactly seven large readable process nodes: Input, Plan, Payload, Generate, Evaluate, Iterate, Output
the label Payload must appear exactly once in the entire diagram
one clear forward main path from Input to Output with no duplicated step names
one explicit feedback loop from Evaluate or Iterate back toward Plan or Generate
visual cues for academic rigor such as paper cards, rubric, and polished figure cards
simple clean layout suitable for documentation
references: Use short English node labels to maximize legibility
Prefer a top-down or left-to-right flow with one explicit feedback loop and one primary reading direction

prompt_pack.prompt:
## Block 1: TITLE & PURPOSE
title: 'Academic Figures MCP Workflow v2'
purpose: Create a strict process flowchart showing the current Academic Figures MCP workflow from scientific input to publication-grade output. This version must explicitly fix the previous duplicate PAYLOAD mistake and present one clean main flow.

## Block 2: LAYOUT
asset_kind: workflow_flowchart_v2
figure_type: flowchart
composition: One clean left-to-right or top-to-bottom process diagram with exactly seven nodes on the main path. The main path should read once through the pipeline. Add only one secondary feedback loop.

## Block 3: ELEMENTS
repo: academic-figures-mcp
exact_main_path: Input -> Plan -> Payload -> Generate -> Evaluate -> Iterate -> Output
hard_constraints: the node label Payload appears exactly once; do not repeat any step name; do not add extra unlabeled nodes; avoid parallel duplicate branches
references: include subtle academic cues such as a paper input card, a rubric or checklist near Evaluate, and a polished figure card near Output

## Block 4: COLOR
palette: academic navy base, teal process arrows, amber emphasis for Generate and Output, warm ivory process cards
style_preset: repo_workflow_flowchart
visual_direction: process clarity first, strict, exact, spacious, premium technical diagram

## Block 5: TEXT
language: English
text_usage: only the seven short process labels above; keep every label large and legible; no repeated labels anywhere

## Block 6: STYLE
style: clean vector-like flowchart, bold simple shapes, excellent readability, documentation-ready
negative_constraints: do not repeat PAYLOAD; avoid clutter; avoid dense explanatory paragraphs; avoid handwritten sketch style; avoid tiny labels; avoid ambiguous arrow crossings

## Block 7: SIZE
canvas: 1536x1024
